heading: Offset relating to certain benefits under the Social Security Act
label: 8349 Offset relating to certain benefits under the Social Security Act
notes: References in TextThe Social Security Act, referred to in text, is , . Title II of the Social Security Act is classified generally to subchapter II (§ 401 et seq.) of chapter 7 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. Sections 203, 214, 215, and 224 of the Social Security Act are classified to sections 403, 414, 415, and 424a, respectively, of Title 42. children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this subchapter, if an individual under section 8402(b)(2) is entitled, or would on proper application be entitled, to old-age insurance benefits under title II of the Social Security Act, the annuity otherwise payable to such individual shall be reduced under this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: is entitled to an annuity under this subchapter; and

children.label: B
children.text: is entitled, or would on proper application be entitled, to old-age insurance benefits under title II of the Social Security Act.

children.label: I
children.text: the old-age insurance benefit which would be payable to the individual for the month referred to in paragraph (2); and

children.label: II
children.text: the old-age insurance benefit which would be so payable, excluding all wages derived from Federal service of the individual, and assuming the individual were fully insured (as defined by section 214(a) of the Social Security Act).

children.label: ii
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the amount of a benefit referred to in subclause (I) or (II) of clause (i) shall be determined without regard to subsections (b) through () of section 203 of the Social Security Act, and without regard to the requirement that an application for such benefit be filed.l

children.label: I
children.text: the old-age insurance benefit to which the individual is entitled (or would on proper application be entitled) for the month referred to in paragraph (2), determined without regard to subsections (b) through () of section 203 of the Social Security Act; andl

children.label: II
children.text: a fraction, as determined under section 8421(b)(3) with respect to the individual, except that the reference to “service” in subparagraph (A) of such section shall be considered to mean Federal service; and

children.label: ii
children.text: may not cause the annuity payment for an individual to be reduced below zero.

children.label: C
children.text: An amount computed under subclause (I) or (II) of subparagraph (A)(i), or under subparagraph (B)(i)(I), for purposes of determining the amount of a reduction under this subsection shall be adjusted under .section 8340 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: based on service of such individual; and

children.label: B
children.text: without regard to section 8345(j), if otherwise applicable.

The operation of the preceding paragraphs of this subsection shall not be considered for purposes of applying the provisions of the second sentence of section 215(a)(7)(B)(i) or the provisions of section 215(d)(5)(ii) of the Social Security Act in determining any amount under subclause (I) or (II) of paragraph (3)(A)(i) or paragraph (3)(B)(i)(I) for purposes of this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: a disability annuity to which an individual described in section 8402(b)(2) is entitled under this subchapter, and

children.label: B
children.text: a survivor annuity to which a person is entitled under this subchapter based on the service of an individual described in section 8402(b)(2),

children.label: A
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), reductions under this subsection shall be made in a manner consistent with the manner in which reductions under subsection (a) are computed and otherwise made.

children.label: B
children.text: Reductions under this subsection shall be discontinued if, or for so long as, entitlement to the similar benefits under title II of the Social Security Act (as referred to in paragraph (1)) is terminated (or, in the case of an individual who has not made proper application therefor, would be terminated).

For the purpose of applying section 224 of the Social Security Act to the disability insurance benefit used to compute the reduction under this subsection, the amount of the CSRS annuity considered shall be the amount of the CSRS annuity before application of this section.
The Office shall prescribe regulations to carry out this subsection.

children.label: c
children.text: For the purpose of this section, the term “Federal service” means service which is employment for the purposes of title II of the Social Security Act and chapter 21 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 by reason of the amendments made by section 101 of the Social Security Amendments of 1983.

children.label: A
children.text: who is subject to this subchapter as a result of any provision of law described in section 8347(), ando

children.label: B
children.text: whose employment (as described in section 8347()) is also employment for purposes of title II of the Social Security Act and chapter 21 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986; ando

the term “Federal service”, as applied with respect to any individual to whom this section applies as a result of paragraph (1), means any employment referred to in paragraph (1)(B) performed after .December 31, 1983
